text.title: JPA Entity and Repository Patterns
text.summary: JPA entity with auditing timestamps and JpaRepository with custom JPQL queries including JOIN FETCH for N+1 prevention.

rule.statement: Use @CreationTimestamp and @UpdateTimestamp for audit fields; initialize collections with new HashSet() using @Builder.Default.
rule.rationale: Audit timestamps are set by Hibernate automatically without lifecycle callbacks. @Builder.Default prevents null collection NPEs when using the builder pattern.

rule.statement: Use JOIN FETCH in JPQL queries to pre-load associations needed in a single call; never load associations in a loop (N+1).
rule.rationale: Each lazy-loaded association triggers an extra SELECT. JOIN FETCH loads the association in the same query, reducing N round-trips to 1.

rule.statement: Always paginate list endpoints; never expose an unbounded findAll() on tables that may grow.
rule.rationale: An unbounded query on a 10M-row table causes full-table scans and OOM. Pageable forces the caller to specify a page size.
